recommended_for: Simple deterministic work, single-subsystem changes, clear write sets, runnable tests
notes: Default for most implementation tasks. Optimized for speed and cost.

recommended_for: Default for most implementation work. Higher-complexity tasks, cross-cutting changes, full-stack wiring, multiple coordinated files
notes: Balanced capability and cost. Use when low-tier fails or task spans multiple subsystems.

recommended_for: Complex reasoning tasks requiring deep code understanding. Use sparingly.
notes: High cost. Requires explicit user authorization unless task genuinely cannot be split.

recommended_for: Highest-complexity tasks. Explicit user request/authorization only.
notes: Premium tier. Use only when gpt-5.3-codex is insufficient.

recommended_for: Documentation, commit messages, human-like writing style. Not optimized for code.
notes: Use for docs and copy, not implementation. Plain and human-sounding output.

recommended_for: Large context requirements. Explicit user authorization only.
notes: Extended context variant. Use only when context window is the limiting factor.

recommended_for: Most capable Claude model. Use for highest-complexity orchestration and planning.
notes: Premium tier. Excellent for planning and architecture decisions, not typical delegation.

recommended_for: Balanced Claude model for general-purpose work.
notes: Good balance of capability and cost for Claude-based workflows.

recommended_for: Previous generation Sonnet. Use 4.6 instead unless specific compatibility needed.
notes: Consider upgrading to claude-sonnet-4-6 for latest improvements.

recommended_for: Fast, cost-effective Claude model for simple tasks.
notes: Fastest Claude model. Good for simple, well-defined tasks.
